titleStmt.title: SII 26.162: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 162. (A.R. No. 154 of 1909.) SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

It records an order of the king addressed to the servants of the temple of Vīrachōḻīśaramuḍaiyār, remitting the taxes such as polivu, poṟpū, elavai, yugavai, and svāmibhōgam in favour of the inhabitants living within the temple premises defined by four specified boundaries.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . konermaikoṇṭān Āḷuṭaiyār śrīcoḻiśvaramuṭaiyār koyilil tevakaṉmikaḷukku nam Olai kuṭutta paricāvatu Innāyaṉār tirumaṭai viḷākam nānkellaiyāvatu paritikulatilakaccaturvvedimaṅkalattukku meṟkum teṟkuccirakkuk kallukku vaṭakkum meṟkuppiḷḷaikkaraikkuk kiḻak
kum perāṟṟukkut teṟkum Innāṉkellaikkuḷḷiruntāriyāvar cilaraiyun teṇṭakuṟṟam maṉṟupāṭu Ārāyumiṭattil Ikkoyilil tapasyarum śrīkāriyañceyvārkaḷun tevarkanmikaḷum Ārān=ta mutal tevar śrīpaṇṭāramāvatākavum maṟṟu tammuṭaiya manaccar pukun=tārāyak kaṭavarallātārākavum nammiṟai
yāy van=ta polivu poṟpū Elavai yukavai cuvāmipokam maṟṟumeppeṟāṭṭi raiyum nāṅkoḷḷātomākavum Immaṭaiviḷākattil tevaraṭiyārai vaiccu koṭin=tāriyāvar cilarivarkaḷukkuk kuṭuttirai teṭappeṟātārākavum Oruvaṉai yum Oruttiyaiyum Ōriṭatte kaṇṭeneṉṟu kāraṇakā kāṭṭit tapasyara
m nāmum metirantu kuṭuppomākavu Ittirumaṭaiviḷākat tirukkun=tatapasyaruntevaṭiyārum Eṟuvaṉa cūṭuvaṉa peṟuvārākavum tevaraṭiyār tapasyar kālaṟaccettār comatevar paṇṭāramāvutākavum maṟṟum Onpatu karaināṭṭup paratecat tevaraṭiyār kālaṟac cettār mu
talum Ittevar śrīpaṇṭāramāvatākavum nam Ōlai kuṭuttom Ivai nantamarānārarpāṟ paṭuttuk kuṭukka Iddharmmam panmāheśvararakṣaiḥ §

tāka Innilattāl vanta Iṟai puravu siddhāyam Elavai Ukavai maṟṟumeppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum koḷḷatomāka nam Ōlaikuṭuttom Ituka
ṇṭu nantamāraṉār parappa te Ivai cempiyaṉ ntiriyeḻuttu panmāheśvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 26.162 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
